Author   would also like to thank  all  his  colleagues  from  SP b U , especially the ones coming from abroad  as well as author’s family  that helped  him  personally  by giving best support to make the paper at this stage ,  and everyone who  have  helped along the way for this study  as well as  you  as a reader   for taking time to read this paper ABSTRACT International Franchises often face challenges when adapting to the local market. It is a fact that local companies are more effective when it comes to sensing and designing strategy for the local market. This study aims to investigate how International Franchisee adapt their strategy to local market as well as determine what factors should be considered during strategy formulation to increase the effectiveness of local market adaptation for franchisees through the case of McDonald’s  and Starbucks . In more detail, the paper investigates whether the  local initiatives  by these companies have an actual impact on the local market and its effectiveness. In this context,  local product initiatives  are defined as the extent to which companies or franchisees  adapt to local market .  To test the hypothesis that  local product initiatives  by  McDonalds   and Starbucks  lead  to higher impacts on the local market, an Interview was done to  middle to top  management levels coming from  global  franchise  that operates in  Asian market . The countr y  that w as  considered in this study  is  Indonesia. Respondents were asked to respond to specific factor sets of market adaptation. Responses were analyzed and checked through  direct observation as well as  their company performance  in Indonesian market . The results showed  positive  effect from pre-defined perceived factors  and local product initiatives  executed  by  McDonald’s  & Starbucks . These results show that defined factors by  this  franchise are likely the essentials for responding to the local market. On this evidence, the concept of  glocalization used  by   McDonald’s  and Starbucks  should  be taken into consideration for companies or franchisees  especially  in food sector  who are entering or operating in the new local market to strengthen their overall strategy. The author Identify the current situation of the IF and take  several  factors  for adaptation. Keywords: International Franchise, globalization, foodservice, glocalization, local market, local product INTRODUCTION Significance The findings of this study will conceal a unique set of factors from glocalization strategy that is used by McDonald’s and Starbucks to excel effectively in the local market. The pressure of competition has demanded business to find alternative strategy besides  promoting  a standardized products that  is already offered by  most of  global franchisee or brands   from  different county origin. McDonalds and Starbucks have shown a successful strategy in winning local audience   by using local product initiatives and   its campaign. In this study, there  will be  set of factors that were considered by these International Franchisee for designing  glocalized  products that capable to meet local taste and preferences especially in difficult market such as Indonesia because of the   Asian  plus  Musli m  culture  along with its  strong local spices and food that exists in the country which influence customers’ expectations from  most  products that are being  offered in  the food industry.   McDonalds and Starbucks are Global Franchise  that  had succe eded  in different market . T he success of their local product initiatives along with its campaign in Indonesian market will  make  the glocalization strategy and its set factors are needed to be looked upon  to perform better in local market .  Research  Background Globalization has been a significant matter for quite some time now. Globalizing simply means making a world scale homogeneous. Globalization between different countries is never going to be the same and will have slight differences. The globalization strategy in International Franchisee has becomes a reliable strategy and becoming a trend everywhere for most International Companies which quite often faced with the option to choose between standardization or adaptation in their strategy. These two strategies have pros and cons for each. Standardization strategy may be used to minimize the costs and bring better brand image. Another strategy which is Adaptation, offers the fill the needs gap of local market and find a better fit for the product. Markets are getting similar each day, some of the preferences, tastes, style becoming universal which made many people support the idea that  in order to  survive, companies should be able to do globalization (Levit t  1983). However, there are some other things that affect the market and  make each of them unique because of cultures, languages, religions, infrastructures, rules, and regulations in it that often gives a problem for globalization. Because of the increase in the globalization and the essential strategy to win the market, Author found the need to clarify on which strategy International Franchise should use and importance between the two strategies. Standardization or Globalization is a key strategy for International Franchisee to excel in local market. The exponential growth in the local market made a lot of Franchisee to went global and expand their market rapidly. A successful IF strategy is based on how good a company develop their strategy on catching similarities and differences which exist in both countries (Isobel and Lowe 1999). External environment may be considered to improve IF strategy in the local context. The overall strategy may not need adaptation if there are lot of similarities between home country and the target market, however when there’s too many differences between these countries, this is when adaptation is obviously needed. Various types of strategy for having a competitive edge in foreign market are  definitely already  exists. In this paper, IF strategy considers three main points namely, the product strategy on how it fits the market needs, distribution strategy on the product to the local market, and IF promotion strategy. The focus of this paper is on the methods for producing and promoting the brand, which gives a signal to the responsible manager about the right product for the market. International Franchisees are often faced a problem when transforming their “global” strategy to local strategy  in order to  be exist for a long time in the market especially in this rapid movement of the market. Thus, the importance of adaptation strategy and possible adjustment in the strategy for the local market should be pointed out.  Franchise Franchising is a set of system from a brand that involves selection of goods distribution  that usually owned by entrepreneurs or known as franchisees. Brand signature, knowledge is provided by franchisors. There is right to profit and certain risk under franchise agreement. Franchising itself is an agreement between the brand or company which give rights to franchisee to use the brand or business expansion which is going to be owned and managed by franchisee with certain percentage fee incur for each good and services it provides.  Franchising  is a can also be categorized as a form of partnership with brand or companies that have good trademarks. The  department and overall business structure has been established well already. Franchisee is the one that use  particular brand’s  trademark, signature, and established system from that brand. The brand or franchisor must provide all aspects in the business such as operation, management, marketing, basically anything that makes the venue operating well where the franchisee will be obliged to pay some fees to the provider.  The partnership confirmation is done through specific license and agreement. Some core elements of franchise include the contract or agreement between  two parties, brand from the goods or service provided, operations of the business managed by the initiator, and the standard measurements system as well as procedures being supervised or go through quality check for certain period by the provider. Each franchise business model  has to  go through the agreement that involves at least both parties, franchisee and franchisor.  The agreement may also be adjusted depending on the nature of the deal that usually influence the required capital, training, managerial issues  and also  the number of the franchise. In  general,  however, the agreement  tends  to be similar because the franchisee is always obliged to meet the payment requirement from the brand company that may be based on the portion of record sales. In return, the initiator of franchisee has the right to provide similar services or products in the market they chose.  In specific case, initiator or franchisee  has to  meet all the requirements like the minimum capital for example before gaining rights to utilize the brand. In many cases, the royalty of each sale will also be transferred to the brand company even though the number  ay  differ. 15% is the maximum royalty percentage that usually be found throughout franchise agreement. In the agreement also includes the address of the franchisee, the type of intellectual property right that they want to utilize covering business operations and its activity, the  rights  and obligations for both parties,  assistance at the initiation stage, area, term of agreement, payment procedure as well as its compensation, ownership, dispute, and termination of the whole agreement.  (Amir, 2013) Literature Review Glocalization concept is an adaptation process of product or services which is provided by International Company that takes local culture and preferences into consideration. Glocalization is a combination of globalization and localization which often found in business world that also mean adaptation of their product or services to local culture . However, glocalization is quite close to Internationalization concept to explain individual, group, organization,  product  or  services that reflect global standardization as well as local acceptance. Glocalization is global strategy that also interprets local value. The origin of the word comes from Japanese words  Dochakuka  which means the adaptation farming technique that combined the local conditions  where later has become marketing strategy that Japan uses to market their product to meet the local preferences or some specific characteristics. This term appears in 1980s in Japanese writer in Harvard Business Review that was written by Roland Robertson, a sociology professor that describes glocalization as a new adaptation concept to local market because of global pressure. He also mentioned that glocalization tends to appear universal as well as centralized at the same time. According to Robertson, the important things in the glocalization  process   is because the world has become more pluralistic,  individual  and basically all local group has the power to adapt, innovate, and maneuver in the  glocalized  world. All process is depended on each other and closely connected. Commodity and media  are  not seen as coercive but more  like  the material provision that will be used in creation by individual or a group in the  glocalized  environment. Glocalization also born because of globalization effect. Globalization concept originally comes from the  western culture that usually focus on expansion which brings penetration to different markets and tend to be accepted by the people from different countries.  The global culture plus the local values will make the combined culture. In this case globalization that was first designed  to be global environment is not fully true because each market and local consumers will differ from one area to another.  Therefore,  when globalization enters a country, the values of that globalization strategy will be combined or at some extent added with local value that brings different culture results in different market.  (Roland Robertson, 2001) In the study that was carried by  Vaccari  in 2011, glocalization focuses on global level while also considering the local preferences without removing the perks which globalization offers.   The  glocal  marketing concepts also has been studied by other researchers such as  Svensson  (2001),  Vignali  (2001), Keegan (2005),  Alimiene  (2008), and Oliveira (2013). The whole concept is still very relevant for discussion  up until today  especially for  understanding local specific.  In the past study conducted by Hokey and  Hyesung  Min, cross-cultural competitive benchmarking for  international  franchisee was analyzed by examining the perceived quality of restaurants, their study reveals that customer impression about service quality differs between countries. Another researcher is Agnes P. Olszewski wh ere the study  focused on the  international  marketing strategy by Fast Food Franchises  because the  fast-food  market has reached maturity stage and franchisees are looking for new opportunities which made move to explore new customers in global marketplace.  Sasu  in 2005 conducted a study for companies that are going to go global or  internationalization that covers product,  market  and company characteristics for means of adaptation.  The study that was conducted by Maria et al (2006) focused on the standardization of franchises to go global.  The comparative study about two franchises doing international expansion was also done by  Arunee  L (2017) coming   from education and service sector. The study touches the selection procedure in domestic and global expansion.  However, there is no comprehensive study about the factors that  actually influence  the  glocal  strategy or  local product  initiatives  which author find interesting to explore  in Indonesian market  and also  will  contribute to the past studies , the external environment will be pointed out in this study  in  tandem with the glocalization initiatives evaluation from the chosen subject. Theoretical Back g round The  theoretical background will  be presented to  better support  analyze empirical data. Adaptation strategy will be discussed which followed by external factors affecting IFs. The  theoretical background  in this study has three main topics. First, the global environment where international franchisees operate in local market being presented and problem which arises from global marketing. Second, the introduction of local adaptation concept or known as glocalization and its alternatives. Third, the international franchisee which focuses on the foodservice sector and implementation of new product from local adaptation or glocalization strategy to deal with local market. Globalization International Franchise strategy has developed from past years and often reflected by the situation at the time. This may bring different results however the core principle remains the same for the market in general. There is always going to be resources, devices, and problems ( Kotabe  and  Helsen , 2009). The current environment has been wrapped with complexities by appearance of delicate machines (Keynes, 1930). International franchisee has always been faced with the issue on getting their global marketing successful. Globalization is always present for big companies and brands agenda especially for the growing market in China, India,  Indonesia  and other countries that are developing quickly which mostly attract their attention to be present in the market. (Wilken and Sinclair, 2011) Globalization has introduced products and reached many people across the globe. The expansion because of standardization applied by companies makes it rapid in terms of production. However,  often times  demand by customer has more characteristics and preference where standardization cannot keep up with the differences even though it is giving abundance options for the customer to choose from ( Kotabe  and  Helsen , 2009). The global product trend and culture does not indicate that all consumers having the same preferences and values which brings researcher many problems when addressing marketing part of standardized products (Holt, Quelch and Taylor, 2004). There are many ways to achieve the global vision, however choosing which path an organization should take is a deciding factor to attain the globalization goal (Deloitte, 2012) Global Marketing According to Levitt (1983), the possible routes for organizations to take usually around how best it serves the marketing activities and to what extent it should be standardized or localized following the specific market characteristics (Duncan &  Ramaprasad , 1995). There is also a recent approach that concentrate on geographic region or also known as regionalization. The concept comes from localization and globalization as well ( Fastoso  &  Whitelock , 2008). The trade-off applied and existed between having local responsiveness and standardization or reaching economies of scale. This is basically the key strategic choice on global strategy ( Ghermawat  2007). Companies can have competitive edge by either focus on local products or focus on standardization.  Kotabe  and  Helsen  stated that most of global products are motivated because of lowering costs from mass production advantage, whereas local products are motivated by market calls to increase overall customer satisfaction that meets their preference and taste. The tradeoff itself between local focus and global efficiency being argued by several researchers. According to Levitt (1983), consumers prefer superior quality and reliability from global products. According to Yip (2003), standardization can increase preference. According to Steenkamp (2003), there is a positive relation between consumers preferences on global products and the perception of global products are sold around the world than being available on a local market. Consumers often has a perception that all global products associated with modernity, efficiency, progress,  consumerism  and a promise of abundance (Holton 2000). Based on study performed by Steenkamp & DeJong’s (2010), it is evident that consumers may look for strong preference for global products and strong preference for local products. However, it is also possible that consumers may reject both local and global products because their perception on companies’ intention on luring them (Arnett,2002). At the end of the day, IFs always try to address both issues efficiently so that there will be no sacrifice on one side or the other.  Glocalization The term glocalization is made from viewpoint of being global and locally blended. (Oxford Dictionary). The core of globalization is highly connected to economic of scale, the word glocalization comes from Japan which has been considered in many businesses (McCreary,2010). Glocalization first introduced by Japanese economists that stated  dochakuka  which means land, arrive and process of. Another idea came from a sociologist who said the adjustment effect of local conditions on global pressures is a good description of glocalization (Robertson, 1997,  Khondker , 2004). In a simple manner,  glocal  is being focused on global but at the same time, considering local preferences greatly and up to date without sacrificing the advantage of global operations ( Vaccari , 2011). It also focuses on the ethnocentric and polycentric orientations (Keegan, 2005) Glocal  Marketing The concept of  glocal  marketing has been studied by many researchers such as  Svensson  (2001),  Vignali  (2001), Keegan (2005),  Alimiene  (2008), Kotler (2009).  Vaccari  (2011), and Oliveira (2013). The concept  still continues  on many discussions and current study because it is very relevant topic when it comes to understanding the local market characteristics and its cultural aspect for decision making on International Franchisee Strategy. Many global companies or brand use globalization strategy, however there are also many global companies that  actually shift  their focus from multi-domestic to be more global that puts boundaries on local market adaptation ( Kotabe  and  Helsen , 2010). There is evidence that in competitive market, global companies are looking for strategy alternative to have that competitive edge. This is how the global marketing combined with local adaptation being adopted and considered as an initiative ( Vaccari , 2011).  Glocal  marketing has several advantages such as there is  actually a  balance and congruence in marketing strategy and operation, IFs  acquire bigger market, consumers feel more connected to the brand covering their local preferences (Kotler, 2009).  The  glocal  strategy is a blend between global and domestic strategy which covers many issues from global perspective, as well as local ones. The board usually gives strategic direction in case of global strategy, whereas local preferences are being handled by local units ( Vinerean , 2010). Glocalization approach can be found on marketing plans, corporate strategies, product initiatives from last decade (Oliveira, 2013). In a simple manner, the glocalization concepts ensure to be global but certain management level must base their strategy to address local preferences or specific characteristics from the chosen market (Kotler, 2009).  Global Brands International Franchisees are mostly global brands that have covered consumers from across the globe, consumer already has perception of specific characteristics for each IFs during their buying decision. A brand that exists in many parts of the world is already pointing credibility by that achievement itself. However, what is needed to keep in mind is that these companies still  have to  follow certain regulations and obligations when performing their activities (Holt, Quelch, and Taylor, 2004). There is a positive effect between being a global brand toward consumer perception,  attitude  and motives. Even though most of consumers see global brands with little importance, it  has  brought  an  effect on their attitudes toward the brand which leads to purchasing attitude as well (Johansson and  Bagozzi  2010). The global brands carry more positive attribute than most people think ( Dimofte  et al. 2010). According to Alden et al, it comes with more quality, better esteem (Johansson, 2005), and perceived to be better or having more advantage than local products (Holt et al, 2004). According to Johansson, by average, quality is the most preferred when it comes to global brand, social responsibility, global  myth  and the rest is not explained. In addition, the word “global brand” is often heard in business language, but the real meaning of it is often misunderstood and the global brand has problem in concept and its metrics (Pitta and  Franzak , 2008). However, the recognition and feasible standardization is still the main association with global brand terms.  There are several key metrics that were determined by Interbrand’s namely, the role of brand in consumer decision making process, its strength and financial performance (Interbrand, 2012) G lobal Brands in Franchisee for Foodservice sector Almost 20% of global brands are coming from food industry including FMCG. McDonalds  and Starbucks are  on  the top 1 00  lists from all the brands across the globe. The standardization mechanism is a good example of these brands. Especially for McDonald’s, there is a specific study about its whole system such as their way of standardization, producing similar products and quality, standard updates and sometimes the term “McDonaldization” appear (Ritzer, 2004). In that study, he also mentioned that McDonalds is often picked for sound example of standardization, however the president of McDonald’s International stated and insisted that the brand focus to be present with local culture as possible and  the majority of  the menu has been adjusted in  glocalized  manner in such a way that new product development from local initiatives or preferences can be made (Ritzer, 2004).  Author would like to emphasize on these brand marketing mix implementations that brings various deciding factors to  take into account  because differences in culture, climate, religion, income. Cultural differences explained by Hofstede (1997) is a sound study to cover the consumer decision making process and their behavior as well as attitude toward the brand. The shift from the  behaviour  of consumer toward certain products especially in food will make global brands or international franchisee consider crucial part of their marketing initiatives or plan. Local Product Development by IFs in Food Sector The global brands have been key examples for any other brands because of their market share achievement and customer satisfaction in general. The innovation they have is divided into category such as reaching new customers with diverse menus, overall experience from start to end, and global expansion. Fast food brand has become major focus in the market (Euromonitor, 2012). The administration system or decision process however, not very  agile  and not responsive to the local environment which brings quite specific preferences. These brands spent not small resources to do market research for new product innovation but still get it wrong (The Open University, 2012) with their market share in global and local that was put at risk ( Dumitrescu , 2010).  Some brands examples that started with standardized products are McDonald’s, Starbucks, Coca-Cola which later adjusted their products to better serve market’s needs & preferences. (Taneja et al, 2012). In specific example for McDonald’s, they tend to keep the standardized operations while at the same time adjust some elements that are necessary to response the local preferences.  However, this does not necessarily mean that other brands should start with same tactics which begin with standardized globalization (Melewar, 2004). They have been doing this for about 10 years now and one that that changed from 10 years ago was that the company start their strategy on regional consideration (Wilken, 2011). There were some examples on how globalization strategy that  actually adapted  their global view and marketing to specific regions or countries with the help of new products development which specifically designed to meet local customer’ needs and preferences. ( Dumitrescu  and  Vinerean , 2010). There are  McBiffana  in Portugal,  McSpaghetti  in Philippines, Maharaja Mac, Veggie Nuggets in India,  Ebi  in Japan,  McLaks  in Norway, &  Uduk  rice in Indonesia. These were some successful examples of adaptation and glocalization strategy usage by McDonald’s.  Other  examples coming from Starbucks are  the tea drink variants they first invented and provided for Chinese market.  However,  there is also a critic coming from Govindarajan (2010) that says about its positive result may fade in the long term because of shift in preferences and design of specific local product initiatives from glocalization strategy. Glocalization may not succeed in quick-preference shift like in certain emerging market and the best approach for companies that already use globalization as their strategy is to do reverse innovation for each local product development they are trying to execute. The reverse innovation takes into consideration the local insight which come from normal people or very likely, potential customers that can give insight to the company at their daily life and in the street.  Problem Identificatio n Choosing between standardization or adaptation has been an issue for strategy of International Franchisees. The home market characteristics may influence on strategy development for the local or foreign market ones which impacting IF overall performance. It is evident that IF cannot separate the business and the social context in the local market such as believes, ethics and values. Therefore, IF must know when to adjust their strategy to fit the local market and its demand. To have a competitive edge in the local market, IF should have good strategy which position their product, overall brand, perception of promotion initiatives and the specific customer characteristics of the local market. This research is dedicated to find out the external factors in the local market and how it affects overall IF strategy. Problem definition Combining the past study regarding glocalization concept, author decide to define problem in this study as follows. Main research question How International Franchisees ad apting   their  globalization strategy to specific local market ? Identifying adaptation mechanisms of IF from foodservice Industry  to specific local market, t his main research question will be divided into  four  sub questions which makes the reader to understand better why adaptation is needed and better explained by sub questions.   Sub research question 1 Why International Franchise invest to create local products? The International Franchise invest to create local products not in small amount resources, there must be a big reason why they invest into such things rather than stick with one strategy that could be implemented in the specific market. In this case, this research question will try to answer the matter which explain why International Franchise decided to go take this route.  Sub research question 2 What factors  o n the  product initiatives  need to be considered for IF on their local market strategy? The IF’s strategy and its policy differ in each country. These Ifs are facing with tons of differences from external environments that have big influence on their strategy. Identifying these factors and analyzing external environment might be a proper way to sharpen IF strategy for local market.  Focusing on factors which exists in the external environment on the local market, will make us better understand how it influences the demand and why it is important for IFs to adapt the current strategy. Sub research question 3 What local adaptations these IFs have made and transformed it to their local product? Author will investigate on IFs such as McDonald’s  and Starbucks  that ha ve   adapted their strategy to local market and made a product,  promotion  and distribution initiatives in Indonesia Sub research question 4 How  the local product adaptation   initiated by International Franchisee affect their performance? In this study, Author would like to analyze and investigate how the impact   really is brought to the company through glocalization implemented by the chosen case s .  Research model Author designs the logic overview of the paper as follows Research Model Research Purpose The purpose of this study is to investigate how International Franchisees adapt its globalization strategy to specific local market and to understand the implementation of localization strategy by these IFs as well as connecting its impact in local market by observing the case of McDonalds and  Starbucks  in Indonesia .  Several research exists in this area but focus on broader topic of the globalization strategy concept, and this paper intends to gather tested theory which will then be compared with one another to give perspective and bring essential information that will allow reader to see how International Franchisee handles the local market  along with  its steps and  benefits by describing  influencing factors  where author also aim to produce a guideline for IF or any other International companies that operates in foreign market to enhance their strategy. Theoretical results will be the contribution to the literature that have not touch the factors on local product development initiatives that was executed by chosen franchisees. The practical results will be the evidence of glocalization strategy and set of factors that  can  be used as a guide for other franchisees or brands when developing a  glocalized  product  in the local market .  In a simple manner, author intents to bring awareness of adaptation strategy in the Global Company and to provide some of the factors that influence them.   Limitations Author will investigate McDonalds  as well as Starbucks  and their strategy on local market. The focus of this research will be food sector industry.   Author will  also  study  McDonald s and Starbucks  Indonesia   which may resulted differently on  other  specific market  characteristics or from another country. Each of which has its own plus and minus depending on the type of research question that will be used, behavior observation, as well as the focus on the current events. Author decided to pick Case study because it does not require some sort of control on the behavior and answer most of the research question from how and why. The following table explains why it is relevant to use case study for this paper Strategy Research question Control over behavior Current situation Survey Who, what, where how many no yes Experiment How, why yes yes Case study How, why no yes Analysis Who, what, where how much no Yes/no History How, why no no Research methodology  comparison The research strategy should be used according to research question type. Case study itself is an  empirical resource  which investigates current phenomenon. It is a specific research strategy where question of how and why is being raised on the current event and investigator has little control (Yin, 2003). The case study increases an in-depth understanding as well (Merriam, 1998). Author believes that in answering research questions on this paper, case study is much more suitable for raising some factors that can best solve the questions or issues on the paper topic. Author would like to firstly understand how international franchisees are adapting to local market and secondly, what factors that affect them. Author also thinks the information that was received to create this paper from different people with direct connections to the International Franchisees such as McDonald’s  and Starbucks  which  will bring data that can be supported by  case study and not the other. The phenomenon that is happening in real life business cases are being observed and analyzed to see the boundaries. Even though case study is less preferred than experiments or survey by researchers because these two strategies use strict steps and easy to follow where case study may not be clear which areas should be more investigated. However, case study can generalize the theoretical proposition which may take a longer time to finalize the findings and many text in the end. The limitation is always present in author mind during paper writing which may work solely on food service sector on the specific market and not for the other business sector, however case study is the appropriate one to perform to better clarify the boundaries. Quality of  R esearch Some measures are put in place to make sure the results quality is not compromised when performing the research. Applicability and dependability of the results were greatly considered. There are four tests that are available to use to ensure quality of the research especially when case study is being used. Reliability, Internal validity, external validity, and construct validity. These are the main four tests which will be described in more details in the next paragraph.  Reliability  is real underlying facts that the method of a study such as data collection may be repeated and will give same results. The aim of it is to minimize errors and biases of a study (Yin, 2003). Reliability also refers when the findings can  actually be  replicated. In a simple manner, if the study being done again the same way it will give same results. Although this can be a problem because human behavior may shift depending on the environment and times which makes it never static (Merriam 1998). To increase reliability for a study, use case study protocol  and develop case study database can be used (Yin, 2003). In this paper, Author tried to point out research approach and how most of the data being collected so that other researchers may repeat the steps and still gaining the same results. Author believes that some other researcher may perform similar topic and will have same results depending on the chosen location and chosen IFs. However, the limitation may still be applied for different industry besides food sector.  Internal Validity  is formulation of causal relationships where certain conditions are pointed out to bring to other conditions which can be distinguished from fake relationship (Yin 2003). Internal validity serves the question on how the findings really match with reality, how it aligns with reality, is the captured findings  actually exists , correct measuring of what researcher is actually measuring. (Merriam 1998). Thus, Internal validity gives a connection to the reality. There are four ways that can be done to increase internal validity namely, pattern matching, explanation building, address rival explanations and logic models. In this paper, Author will try to form internal validity by producing logic pattern of the problem areas which are real and can be concluded.  External Validity  focuses more on the generalization of the findings. External validity explains if the findings from a study can really be applied to other situations which means general results of the study. (Merriam, 1998) To increase external validity, use of theories in case study must be properly addressed (Yin, 2003). Author believes that this study is valid for other IFs that are operating in different local market than their home country  such as  in Indonesia .  The influence of external environment and characteristics of the market still plays a great role and may be applied to other industry besides food sector and give similar results, however, not everything is going to bring the same results obviously depending on the product types and preferences.  Construct validity  is the creation of operational measures for the concepts that are being studied (Yin, 2003). Recent critics about case study is when researcher fails to develop a sufficient operational set of measures and subjective judgements. In this case, there are three ways to increase construct validity when performing case study namely, multiple sources of evidence during data collection, establishing chain of evidence, and prepared draft report which is reviewed by key respondents or informants. In this paper, Author tried to use all the previously mentioned ways to increase validity because author used multiple sources, interviewees from specific IFs, as well as relations of evidence for the conclusions of the research. Basic  Institution model Global brands strategy is heavily influenced by rules that comes from the company itself but also the rules that apply outside of the company ( Jannson , 2007). The mode l which was developed by Jansson in 2007 explain the societal institutions related to organizational area that  actually affect  global brand and their strategy. It is a strategy that involves market that allows business to find a better combination of different aspects to get better outcomes. In marketing mix, it is quite common to know 4P which covers product, price,  place  and promotion. Additionally, the other “P” can include People, physical evidence, process  which is labelled as 7P altogether. Product Product is a component of the product that includes design and product development to market with some adjustment for the product itself or services depending on the business. Product is a company offering aimed at fulfilling customer needs and wants. In this context, product can be goods or services being offered to potential customers for satisfying their needs and wants. Product Strategy  in this case is to determine and provide a  right product  for the targeted market  so that it fills the gap or market niche resulting increase in IF revenues. As for some factors that are considered for product creation is quality, packaging, alternatives or substitutes, brand, size, type,  warranty  and services. In Marketing mix, product strategy is an important element that can influence other marketing strategy. A type of product that will be produced and marketed will determine promotion activities which also impacting price positioning along with its distribution. Price Price is a management system in a company that will determine the right price for a product or services that is already thought about some discount scenarios, distribution costs and some other variables which will contribute to the end-product price. The element of revenues  touch  very much price aspect in marketing mix.  In determining price, there  are some direct and indirect factors. The direct  factor  for price includes raw material,  production cost, marketing cost, regulations costs and some other factor that directly contributed to the price. The indirect factor is a product price which competitors offer that may be considered as a substitute or some discount that exist that will give some weight to consumer options. Place In distributing the goods or carrying out the services for the market, a place is needed for the whole flow to operate  like sending or purchasing the goods from the clerk. The ease of access for reaching the service provider or goods can be a critical factor for the franchise itself. The decision about physical location of a franchise will be a medium for most of the strategy that is being used by the brand to be able to reach the consumer coming from different locations.  Promotion Promotion is an activity to communicate or reveal products advantage and persuade customers in a positive way to buy the product. In other words, promotion is an activity to introduce a product from a particular company, brand, franchise to the market so that the product will be officially launched and giving awareness for the public. Promotion is quite often considered as a main component of marketing.  With this promotion, consumers or potential customers will know that such products  exist  which may be a  better option  than what current market offers. Promotion is an  element that can be used to introduce the product or services to the market through the means of advertising, direct selling, sales  promotion  or publication. The last one for promotion is direct marketing which basically mean establishing direct relations with targeted customers to collect feedbacks, opinion, satisfaction or even improvement and suggestions to have a  long-lasting relation  which is a customer loyalty. People People is all the actors that involves in the product or services starting from its preparation until the product has been delivered to customers which also quite often bring influence  on  customers perception. Process Process is a procedure, mechanisms, or flow activity to deliver product or services. This element processes  have  a heavy meaning  in delivering the product. Physical Physical evidence is a factor that can influence consumer decision to buy or use the product and services being offered. In some countries, McDonald’s was a market leader in food service category  but in some other may not be the case.  There are some other fast food or local restaurants that has foundation on par with McDonald’s. These restaurants should be kept in mind because they are the actual threat in McDonald’s environment. Especially in recent times, supermarkets have been also providing take away meals which also puts the competition even tighter.  Mr. Henri also said that while retaining the position in the market, it has been a main challenge ever since covid breakout started. As you now that first McDonald’s Franchise in Indonesia located in  Sarinah  was also closed for a few months before it operates back again. However, we overcome it and made it to this point.  The McDonald’s value has  merged with my identity as marketing  manager,  and I always think like McDonald’s person when addressing things in  the  company. He also emphasized on the importance of local integration to the brand at the strategic level which brought brand perceptions  to all customers. There were some initiatives that McDonald’s initiated brought from higher ups which plan to increase local perception and McDonald’s position that could integrate into Indonesians lifestyle.  It was very clear that brand strategy positioning of our products which you may see on the current menu ( Uduk  rice,  McFlurry   Markisa , Burger rendang, Burger  balado ) as  well as the advertisement we use for Indonesia market based on the acquired data. The  most  recent one was the BTS meal that appeared to be  one of  the most successful collaboration  initiatives   that brought massive impact on sales in Indonesia so fa r . Around 135 suppliers come from Indonesia market  directly and indirectly. Most of the chicken suppliers are from Indonesia as well as vegetables which we serve in some of our menus. Mr. Henry also stated that they are aiming and will continue to form relationships with these suppliers and produces as well as spices. When it comes to Indonesia, the food must contain specific spices otherwise it will not enter their taste. He emphasized a lot about the national production bodies such a s  Rekso  National  (RNF) ,  Cahaya   Gunung  Foods  and  others .  The strategy that McDonald’s Indonesia use from the beginning is still the same which is not sacrificing their global leader but also addresses local importance such as local preferences and characteristics. This has been the key for McDonald’s to be able to penetrate Indonesia to reach market share where we are now ever since that strategy picked up.  Not only McDonald’s Indonesia put many variances to its menu which also concern about the expectation of Indonesian preferences and taste, but also integrating and introduce different products and services such as recent partner with  BlueBird . By this strategy we could see that local suppliers will have many opportunities to better supply their products that will contribute to the McDonald’s success which also impacting their success which create ripple effect to other partners, so win-win solution for all sides.   McDonald’s Indonesia also participated in big events each year like long holidays  Ied  Fitr or Christmas where we provide the environment for the family to maximize their experience in our restaurant.  This  movement has made our brand being acknowledged by local consumers because of the efforts we took and some of the partial partnerships with producers to meet Indonesian’s preferences and taste.  We are very thankful that these partners have continued to be with us all this year that made us where we are right now. Apart from the local partnerships we have, McDonald’s Indonesia has employed 14.000 people in 200 restaurants across Indonesia which mostly managed by local as we all may know Bambang  Rachmadi  was the first person to open McDonald’s restaurant in Indonesia but also first local to manage the restaurant, and we have been following this example.  Around 85% basic ingredients come from local suppliers directly from Indonesian farmers so we can ensure food we serve is very fresh with standard quality. Local products initiatives at McDonald’s Indonesia There are some examples of local products initiatives from McDonald’s Indonesia that has proved successful strategy in reaching local taste. The  “ Ini  rasa  kita ”  project are best example of McDonald’s resources allocation for local market. Their aim was to improve the local taste by integrating it with the brand that led successful initiatives.  From the start to end of the project creation, it was designed and applied by McDonald’s Indonesia team with the help of its partners such as local suppliers to  actually realize  the specific dishes and meet local needs.  The  Ini  Rasa Kita project has turned into fix menu for quite long time since its launched in 2017 because local tastes can be found under this category such as burger  balado , rendang and satay.  Apart from the main dish like burger and  uduk  rice , McDonald Indonesia also launched  McFlurry   Rujak  and  McFlurry   Markisa . During specific season or event, McDonald’s offer something like beef green sauce during Ramadhan and the real issue about launching such menu was because the products only exist for limited time, thus the supplier and sales also limited within that timeframe which  makes it under the short life category. However, the enthusiasm of the local consumers  was  so positive especially for those limited time offer (LTO) products.   McFlurry   Rujak  &  Markisa   actually designed  for mainly female customers that also brought authentic tropical fruit from Indonesia,  Markisa , whereas Burger Rendang was a dish that target all Indonesian customers because its well-known unique recipe for traditional dish across Indonesia. We also made few adjustments like burger itself has never been there with “rendang” and the meat was  actually burger  meat which is soft and juicy so people could enjoy it in a bite without much trouble and still giving same taste as the traditional dish rendang provides where the actual dishes require much more effort to enjoy.  It took us around 7 months to  actually being  able to deliver and put this menu in our restaurant  including  its  campaign preparation.   There is specific resource allocated to ensure the launch of local product initiatives along with campaign successful. Even though the creation of local product under certain conditions and standard, there is a room to try things at national level depending on the conditions of the logistics complexity as well. The team from McDonald’s Indonesia that involve in specific project for local product usually  had enough flexibility to try the recipe with suppliers, ingredients and some other things that could make the product better. Rest assured that these process of trying this should follow certain procedures according to our global standards, quality, safety and so on.  McDonald’s Indonesia had certain flexibility and authority to decide for the  glocal  projects which often involve the brand perception as well as goals. We always integrate our local initiatives  product with marketing campaign as you may see from all our local products that focus on national area.  As for the steps to realize the project, it usually starts with the concept creation followed by suppliers’ feasibility, recipes experiment, research the market and launch it.  The team that is responsible for project realization is from product development from local branch.  In terms of the price determination for these types of products, we adjust it on the purpose of the campaign, if it is for penetration then we will lower the price and make a specific bundle menu, if it is premium local product then obviously, we set a higher price. These initiatives have brought our brand to appear more local and being  acknowledged  on the local market.  The integration local products and touch in our restaurant has been very successful thanks to the approved investments from higher ups to address such things. Not only thing enhance our presence in local markets, but also the brand perception in general also has increased.   We also notice each time we launched these initiatives, local customers had never been enthusiastic about the brand because the additional dishes we add in the menu brings their Identity as a local person that could  actually connect  with our brand which is recognized globally in the modern environment. This can only be done through local products initiatives and not by any other strategy.  The Marketing Manager also stated McDonald’s Indonesia using similar metrics for local products evaluation and performance check which includes revenue, brand perceptions or engagement in general. There was no failure in local products initiatives, probably  only  small drawbacks from one product which was under limited time offering  plan. However up until now, the objectives have been met with different levels of achievement and contributing to brand prosperity. The project “ Ini  Rasa Kita” was one of the best local launches that we did that brought huge positive impact toward the brand even though the sales are not as many as our usual fries, but  definitely brought  good impact. The  McFlurry   M arkisa  and  Rujak   was planned for short cycle goals, however when we look at the impact it brought, we decided to make those two stays on the menu which probably we will be adding more in the future with more variations of local taste. Burger Rendang &  Balado ,  Kremes  Chicken was planned for  limited time only according to local events like Ramadhan or Independence anniversary, however it became another main dish added to the menu because of the flow it brings to sales and brand perception. Mr. Henri also said that what matters the most of what should be on the menu is the impact or potential of a product to all  aspects of the business as well as its operation process. These products initiatives were considered medium- and long-term strategy  because we still  have to  make sure we do not lose our global identity. Burger Rendang,  Uduk  Rice, and Chicken  Kremes  only exists in Indonesia  that bring local taste and make customers perception of the product to feel it like McDonald’s products not some other local restaurants products which is quite important for the campaign itself. Societal & Organizational field of Indonesia Indonesia is one of the largest countries in south-east Asia located in Pacific Ocean that has a lot of mountains, rain forests, swamps and possess around 13000 islands as a country. They declared their independence on August 17, 1945, from Japan however the sovereignty was transferred in 1949 approved by Netherlands. Around eighty percent of population is  Muslim  with large volume of trading from different countries that includes products like gasoline to textile.  Indonesia adopts constitutional republic and presidential which makes the president is the head of the state,  government  and commander of National armed forces. The  president usually  serves  between two to five years because of the country regulations.  The regulations come from Roman-Dutch law. The constitution was restored in 1959. The GDP growth  has increased relatively over the year from $811 billion 2006 to $915 billion 2008 which has reached $1,058 trillion in 2020. Most of their GDP comes from agriculture (14%), service sector (37%), and Industrial (48%). Population in Indonesia has been steadily increasing  and ranked number 5 from the world with total of 240 million people. The total life expectancy rate is 70 years where 50% of the population  live in urban areas. It is also considered as nation with young age because the median age is 28 years. 90% of the population are literate. Indonesia is a multi-culture and religious country.  The technological factor in Indonesia has been growing having 140 airports, landlines decent usage. In terms of environmental aspect, the geographic location is a bit difficult because it  is located in  South-Eastern Asia between Indian Ocean and Pacific Ocean. The weather usually hot and humid. 669 languages with over 1100 different dialects are spoken. Indonesia is the biggest market in Southeast Asian area which is one of the reasons the country is included in G20 countries because of fast developing market it has.  Indonesia is also  the part of ASEAN  nations  which  empowers  and has brought influential change to  most southeast Asian nations after signing declaration for commitment in 1967. The culture itself has big influence on the local product initiatives that is on another level besides the other factors, however the campaign  has to  be created in such a way that it keeps the originality of the IF products, so the global presence or image is not lost along the way.  Culture is a complex  matter  in society that bring specific response from people to different stimulus (Dumitru, 2001). It is resulted because of group behaviors, organization,  ideology  or beliefs. It is also the reason of purchasing factors such as individualism, materialism, moral or religious beliefs ( Nistorescu , 2008). According to Constantin  Sasu , Culture is the legacy from society that was transferred from speech,  literature  and communication. The nature of it is conservative that could only be acquired or inherited from past generations. It can also be the reason for distinctive life of a country. Society or individual may benefit from culture because it can facilitate activities much easier that involve interaction with another human. Culture resides in one’s consciousness and learn it from socializing ( Cateora , 2006). International Franchise must be aware of the cultural variables that can affect the decision of any initiatives to better perform in local market like local product initiatives and its campaign. The major elements in culture are language, institutions, material and symbolic. The first element of culture is Language for written and spoken as well as nonverbal communication. The second element is Institutions that includes the education, attitudes social organization and thought patterns. The third element is Material which includes technology, aesthetics, possessions, proximity. The fourth element is symbolic where religion, tradition and customs resides in the country). The differences in culture will affect brands to adapt to the market due to behavior from the consumer is strongly related to their culture ( Usunier  and Lee, 2009). Some products in the market may have less influence in culture which leads firm to adjust mildly to local market for example technology that is being used and accepted all over the world or chemicals. Food products however require more adjustment because of cultural influence  and specific characteristics from the local market which has shaped consumer behavior or their buying decision. RESULTS The results section will include all four sub questions with intention answering the main research question supported by all the data, material, past research that are relevant for this study to display the glocalization strategy adoption or local product initiatives in the food industry as well as  its influence on customer perception over the brand RQ 1   Why International Franchise investing to create local products? In the fast-paced changing market we are experiencing, the preference for consumption also changes because of the environment. Most of International Franchisee focus on their revenues by having more quantity instead of putting more focus to quality enhancements . It is common for the company to move out from status quo by having more branches with the help of standardization process that incorporates economies of scale and scope to stay long term in the market.  The implementation of glocalization strategy is one of the alternatives for brands to  actually having  more advantage or keeping the competitive edge from local competitors ( Vaccari , 2011).  During this study, author found out that global marketing strategy with local  matters   integrated  to the strategy will bring benefits to the brand,  c ustomers  and  revenues in general. The higher ups from McDonald’s International had stated that they would make sure to continue or increase the effort by adding more frequency as well as penetration rate which addresses local market specific characteristics.  Even though  most of  global brands have always put standardization as their main strategy  and got their aimed market share, however it gets more difficult when the situation asks the franchise or brand to succeed in different market characteristics or known as local market because the global strategy and its perks standardization  does  not always bring optimal outcome in local market (Taneja, 2012).  Some of the brands  actually adapted  standardized process to have their products integrated in their global strategy often adjust their products  according to  their customer needs.  The glocalization  strategy,  however, will make  facilitate  the brand to have global presence but at the same time appearing as a local brand which means  addressing local preferences as well as participating in local events or special moment at national level by having standardization as its core process for global brand or local product initiatives  ( Vaccari , 2011). Multinational companies often get to adjust their main products or services to excel in the market. The glocalization strategy will touch local marketing as well as global marketing which  exclusively strengthened each other campaign that could reach or meet the objectives  from  such marketing activities.  It also allows franchises to perform better in different market characteristics or local specific preferences at the same time give the buyer experience from International Franchisee or brand.  The glocalization strategy is used by global brands as one of the alternative ways to increase market share as well as its penetration rate in local market which quite often competitive because of the global presence they carry.  RQ 2 What factors on the  macro level   that  need s  to be considered for IF on their local market strategy? According to this study, Author could identify several factors  from McDonalds and Starbucks evidence as an American franchise that operates  in Indonesia that has influence  their glocalization strategy.  The influence  are   not  always equal at the same level because some factor such as culture affect more than  political for example . Where the economic situation of a country influence pricing and operation activities of McDonald’s  and Starbucks  Indonesia. The purchasing power and attitude toward the product influence the consumption level which makes study franchisee to adjust accordingly. Steady economic growth in Indonesia also has helped McDonalds to increase their market share because of consumption spending in Indonesia.  These  market characteristics  factors have influenced the sales of chosen IF in general. The relationship with local suppliers to launch local products initiatives also influenced by  the rest of the factors .  Significantly, culture that resides in Indonesia has been the factor that made local product initiatives by the IF to be accepted and preferred, simply because the preferences of the local customers about local taste, recipe, and environment. Author may draw conclusion from the   through  the analysis of factors set from  c ase comparison  regarding the factors that should be considered  during  glocalized  products creation.  RQ 3 What local adaptations these IFs have made and transformed it to their local product? In this study, the strategy of the studied franchisee has evolved to  glocal  product  initatives  that covers the campaign, packaging, local products or recipe, suppliers, as well as distribution to specific market. In the case study, the  brand impact , suppliers , quality  products  and customer experience  are the  factors that were considered  in terms of adaptation. The campaign for  each  local initiatives had to be thought and planned carefully because the timing of the events to appear at national level. The population of Indonesia also influence the speed and effectiveness of the campaign which was shown by “ ini  rasa  kita ” example. The slogan has become popular in the network during the events that made some of those bundle products stay on the menu. The route that McDonald’s Indonesia take has defined  the brand as a local one that also acts as a global brand. There was a specific team assigned to local product initiatives from its preparation until launch. The availability of recipes and product making process was also confirmed with the local suppliers to ensure that the initiatives launch successfully.   In other words, McDonald’s Indonesia has combined their standardized product such as big mac with local food and turned it into Rendang burger .  Starbucks was also done the same thing for their Java chip Frappuccino that has become bestseller for a long time in Indonesian market.  This has transformed the way they deal with the local market to gain a competitive edge. RQ 4 To what extent the local product adaptation initiated by International Franchisee affect their performance? The interview author conducted with IF global brand that operates in local market,  McDonalds  and Starbucks   Indonesia,  showed that local consumers perceive the brand  with the concern of quality of the product, menu variations in terms of  glocal  products in International Franchisee, & appealing environment it provides. McDonald’s Indonesia Marketing manager representative  stated that the objectives have been met thanks to glocalization strategy along with its campaign tied to specific item or bundle. In the result of depth interview, the Marketing Manager said that almost  all of  the  glocal  initiatives had brought positive impact to the brand in general, either how the customer  sees  or perceive the brand or facilitated the brand to actually being more welcomed for local market which resulted  a n  increase  of penetration rate.  The marketing manager also said that most of local product’s initiatives had brought attention of older customers with range of 35-60 where they usually just accompany their kids or family to buy normal menu. This is crucial for them because the target age customer just widen the initial intention of local product initiatives at national level.  In case of Starbucks, the  glocalized  products have allowed them to connect to customer and give better experience for the buyer due to some instilled values that it brings such as  pride of bestselling beverage comes from their origin island java. This has resulted positive attitude for the brand that increase the sales in general. MAIN RQ How International Franchisees adapting its globalization strategy to specific local market? After answering all previous sub questions, it is shown that  Glocalized  products allow International Franchisee to increase their penetration rate in the local market whereas standard product  would not be able to do  for reaching another level of connection with the local customers . The result also showed that the local initiatives impacted positive brand perception from the local customer.  The International Franchise has adapted their strategy into known as  glocalized  strategy that covers the marketing campaign  and local product  initatives  specifically prepared and launched for the local market.  The perceived factors that were determined by the two franchises have proved their successful  glocalized  products launch where each of which has adapted their globalization strategy to  glocalized  strategy to operate in Indonesian market as shown in  both of the cases . CONCLUSIONS The purpose of this study  wa s to investigate how International Franchisees adapt its globalization strategy to specific local market and to understand the implementation of  g localization strategy by  chosen  IF   as well as connecting its impact in local market . Author decided to  analyze  Glocal  initiatives that were executed by McDonald’s  and Starbucks  Indonesia  to find out the perceived success factors when creating a local product development for the specific market like Indonesia.   The theoretical background and data collected from  McDonald’s  and Starbucks  Indonesia  have  shown that the  glocal  strategy is an alternative strategy implemented by International Franchisee in the competitive market that will allow them to increase penetration rate that leads to increase their market share   as a whole which  most importantly create stronger connections with their customers. The local culture influences local product development from the chosen IF which had to be prioritized during product initiation stage. The local suppliers were also considered a factor at which IF could explore their  “ standardized ”  product t o  be combined with local taste  to win local customers and performing better in the local market. Local products initiatives  in general  have  brought  positive attitude towards the brand for the local customers. On this statement author could conclude that International Franchisee can still have a global presence and fostering  relationship for local customers in the market which gives the brand the unique advantage  where local brands and franchises would not be able to do so. This study intends to contribute to knowledge that has been developed in past studies about glocalization strateg y, its  concept  and the local product initiatives creation . The implementation by the chosen International Franchise w ere  analyzed and   check ed for  the impact to the brand  is valid  as well as  to the  local customers.
